output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here's How to Fight Back (Fast)
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your rights and send a legal demand letter in 60 seconds for just $19.99. No signup needed.
content_outline: Why Landlords Wrongfully Withhold Security Deposits
What Your Landlord Can (and Cannot) Legally Deduct
State-by-State Deadlines: How Long Is Too Long?
How to Write a Security Deposit Demand Letter That Actually Works
The Fast, No-Signup Fix: Get Your Letter in 60 Seconds
key_points: Normal wear and tear (scuffs, faded paint, nail holes) is not a valid deduction in any state.
Most states require landlords to return security deposits within 14 to 30 days after move-out.
A formal demand letter citing state statutes is the fastest, most effective first step before small claims court.
Deposits-Back.com generates state-specific security deposit demand letters for $19.99 with no signup or subscription.
output.cta: Don't let your landlord keep what's yours. Generate your state-specific security deposit demand letter at Deposits-Back.com in 60 seconds. Just $19.99. No sign up. No subscription. Get your money back now.
output.target_audience: Frustrated U.S. renters whose landlords are wrongfully withholding, deducting from, or refusing to return a security deposit, actively searching online for legal remedies, tenant rights, and demand letter templates.

short_form_scripts.hook: Your landlord has 30 days to return your deposit. It's day 31. Now what?
short_form_scripts.body: Most states give your landlord a strict deadline. If they miss it, they're breaking the law. But arguing over text won't help. You need a formal demand letter that cites your state's actual statutes. That's exactly what Deposits-Back.com does.
short_form_scripts.cta: For $19.99, send a legally sound letter in 60 seconds. No signup. No subscription. Tap the link.

short_form_scripts.hook: They charged me $800 to repaint the walls. That's normal wear and tear, Karen.
short_form_scripts.body: Your landlord cannot deduct for scuffed floors, faded paint, or small nail holes. Those are normal. If they're wrongfully withholding your deposit, a dispute letter puts the law on your side and shows you mean business.
short_form_scripts.cta: Get your money back at Deposits-Back.com. $19.99. Done in under a minute.
